output.final_deliverable:
I searched for recent news on “artificial intelligence regulation” and located two search-result items (both from The New York Times) in the merged results. Evidence available from the merged results: 

1) Headline (as returned in search results): "Backed by Anthropic, a Super PAC Group Begins an Ad Blitz in Support of A.I. Regulation - The New Yo"
   URL: https://www.nytimes.com/2026/02/23/technology/ai-pac-ad-blitz.html
   Source (from URL): The New York Times
   Available fields from merged results: headline (truncated), URL, inferred source domain.
   Missing fields: publication date (full confirmation), full headline text (search result truncated), main topic / key point (article body not available in merged results).

2) Headline (as returned in search results): "Defeat of a 10-Year Ban on State A.I. Laws Is a Blow to Tech Industry"
   URL: https://www.nytimes.com/2025/07/01/us/politics/state-ai-laws.html
   Source (from URL): The New York Times
   Available fields from merged results: headline, URL, inferred source domain.
   Missing fields: publication date (full confirmation), main topic / key point (article body not available in merged results).

Notes about the search/visit attempts in the merged results:
- The merged results show that visit attempts to read article content failed (visit nodes were missing valid URLs / visits did not succeed). Because of that, I cannot extract publication dates, article summaries/main points, or confirm full headlines beyond the truncated strings returned by the search results. 
- Both found items in the merged results point to the same news website domain (nytimes.com). The user requested visiting at least two different news websites (not Wikipedia); that requirement is not satisfied by the evidence provided.

What is missing (and required to complete the task as requested):
- Successful article page retrievals (full article pages) for at least two news articles from at least two different reputable news websites (not Wikipedia). From those pages I need the publication dates and the article text or leading paragraphs to extract the main topic/key point.

Suggested next steps I can take if you want me to continue (requires successful page visits):
- Retry visiting the two NYT URLs above to extract full headlines, publication dates, and main points. (I already have their URLs from the search results.)
- Perform a fresh search and visit at least one additional different news website (e.g., Reuters, BBC, The Guardian, Washington Post, Bloomberg) and retrieve at least two article pages on AI regulation from different domains, then extract the requested fields and provide a comparative summary.

Cited URLs (from merged results):
- https://www.nytimes.com/2026/02/23/technology/ai-pac-ad-blitz.html
- https://www.nytimes.com/2025/07/01/us/politics/state-ai-laws.html
output.action_summary: I located two search-result items about AI regulation, both pointing to The New York Times (URLs above). The merged results, however, do not include the full article pages because the visit attempts failed, so I could not extract publication dates or the articles' main topics/key points. Also, both items are from the same news website, so I cannot yet compare perspectives across different outlets. To complete the task as requested I need successful article page retrievals (including at least one article from a different news website); once those pages are available I will extract headline, publication date, main topic/key point, source name, and provide a comparative summary.

merged_results.title: Search: Recent news (past year) on "artificial intelligence regulation"

result.query: artificial intelligence regulation news past year site:news OR (site:nytimes.com OR site:reuters.com OR site:theguardian.com OR site:washingtonpost.com OR site:bloomberg.com OR site:bbc.com) "artificial intelligence regulation"
result.intent: Find recent (within the last 12 months) news articles about AI regulation across major news outlets so we can pick at least two different websites to visit and extract headline, date, main topic, and source.
vector_context: Synthesize extracted facts and compare perspectives (post-visit think step)

results.title: Backed by Anthropic, a Super PAC Group Begins an Ad Blitz in Support of A.I. Regulation - The New York Times
results.description: Anthropic, which said this month that it had poured $20 million into the group, has taken a different approach from much of the A.I. industry in calling for tough regulation of the technology it is creating. Public First was formed last year to battle other super PACs backed by the leaders and investors of the rival A.I.

results.title: Defeat of a 10-Year Ban on State A.I. Laws Is a Blow to Tech Industry
results.description: There are no federal laws regulating A.I. but states have enacted dozens of laws that strengthen consumer privacy, ban A.I.-generated child sexual abuse material and outlaw deepfake videos of political candidates.

results.title: Don’t be fooled. The US is regulating AI – just not the way you think | Sacha Alanoca and Maroussia Lévesque | The Guardian
results.description: At first glance, today’s artificial intelligence policy landscape suggests a strategic retreat from regulation. As of late, AI leaders such as the US have doubled down on this messaging. JD Vance champions AI policy with a “deregulatory flavor”. Congress considered a 10-year ban on state ...

results.title: Hochul Reaches Deal on A.I. Regulation in New York - The New York Times
results.description: Hochul said in a statement, adding that the reforms represent progress “as the federal government lags behind, failing to implement common-sense regulations that protect the public.” · The bill, the Responsible Artificial Intelligence Safety and Education, or Raise, Act, is the first A.I.-related state law to be enacted since President Trump signed an executive order last week aimed at reining in states’ ability to limit the artificial intelligence industry.

results.title: Reuters AI News | Latest Headlines and Developments | Reuters
results.description: Artificial intelligence lab Anthropic has no intention of easing its usage restrictions for military purposes, a person familiar with the matter said on Tuesday, adding that the two sides continue to talk following a meeting to discuss its future with the Pentagon.

results.title: Trump signs executive order aimed at preventing states from regulating AI | US news | The Guardian
results.description: Republicans earlier this year failed to pass a similar 10-year moratorium on state laws that regulate AI as part of Trump’s One Big Beautiful Bill Act, with the Senate voting 99-1 to remove that ban from the legislation.

results.title: How A.I. Money Is Flooding Into the Midterm Elections - The New York Times
results.description: Brad Carson, who runs that nonprofit group, Public First Action, said that A.I. donations to state efforts were “largely to stymie the growing backlash on data centers.” The A.I. industry’s spending on federal elections, he said, was meant to influence whether the federal government should have greater power over A.I. regulation, versus letting states do their own regulating. “Everyone knows this is what’s being pushed, so political donations are spiking,” Mr. Carson said. Some donors with ties to the A.I. industry may have an interest in politics that goes beyond artificial intelligence.

results.title: South Korea’s ‘world-first’ AI laws face pushback amid bid to become leading tech power | South Korea | The Guardian
results.description: South Korea has launched what it calls ‘world-first’ laws aimed at regulating artificial intelligence.

details.intent: After visits complete, synthesize the extracted fields into a concise comparison of perspectives across the visited articles.

target_output_fields.comparison_summary: 3-6 sentences comparing perspectives, noting agreements, differences in emphasis (e.g., legal/regulatory progress vs. industry caution), and any notable quoted stakeholders.

details.synthesis_plan: 1) Confirm extracted facts for both articles. 2) Identify the main angle of each (policy progress, criticism, enforcement, economic impact, technical risks, etc.). 3) Note whether articles emphasize government action, industry reaction, civil society concerns, or technical details. 4) Produce a short comparative summary highlighting similarities and differences and an explicit statement of which aspects differ (tone, recommended action, focus).
verification_steps: Ensure all fields are sourced from article pages (not search snippets) and cite the article URLs.
If any data is missing (e.g., paywall blocking content), note what was unavailable and base the summary on verified fields only.
details.rationale: A think/synthesis step consolidates extracted factual outputs into the required comparative summary. This is a reflection/aggregation step and should run after the visit nodes complete so it has definitive data to compare.

details.intent: Find recent (within the last 12 months) news articles about AI regulation across major news outlets so we can pick at least two different websites to visit and extract headline, date, main topic, and source.
target_facts: Short summary/main topic or key point (1-2 sentences)
details.extraction_plan: Return search result snippets (title, snippet, URL) prioritized by recency and relevance. Tag results by domain to ensure at least two different news websites are captured. Mark any paywalled items in snippets so we can avoid or note access issues.
verification_steps: Confirm publication date in each article page (visit).
Cross-check headline and publisher between search snippet and article page.
Prefer articles that explicitly mention policy names (e.g., 'EU AI Act', 'US AI Bill of Rights', government regulation proposals) to ensure topical relevance.
details.rationale: A broad search across major news sites will produce candidate article URLs to visit. Searching first reduces guesswork and ensures visits target actual, recent articles from distinct news publishers (requirement: at least 2 different news websites). 37e8d30e-fad9-4ab7-8b80-30b522191919.title: Visit: Read Article A (first selected search result) and extract required fields

details.intent: Open the top relevant recent news article found by the search and extract the headline, publication date, main topic/key point, and source name.

target_facts.headline: string (exact headline text)
target_facts.publication_date: ISO date string or article-provided date
target_facts.main_topic: 1-2 sentence summary (policy angle, e.g., regulation passage, controversy, proposed rules)

details.extraction_plan:
From the article page extract:
1) The article headline (H1 or displayed title).
2) The publication date (article metadata or byline).
3) The lead paragraph(s) that express the main topic/key point and produce a 1-2 sentence distilled summary.
4) The publisher name from page header or metadata.
Also capture the article URL, author (if present), and any explicit mention of the jurisdiction/policy name for context.
verification_steps: Ensure the headline exactly matches the H1/title on the page.
Confirm the date is within the past 12 months; if not, discard and pick a different result.
If content is behind a paywall, note that and attempt to extract metadata from search snippet or publisher metadata.
Record the article's canonical URL to prevent duplicates.
details.rationale: A direct visit is required to verify publication date, capture the authoritative headline and extract the main topic. This reduces uncertainty from snippet-only data and fulfills the mandate to visit news websites (not Wikipedia). Vector DB will be consulted to see if we already have cached content for this URL.
details.parent_goal: Search for recent news articles about 'artificial intelligence regulation' from the past year. Visit at least 2 different news websites (not Wikipedia) and extract: (1) The headline of each article, (2) The publication date, (3) The main topic or key point, (4) The source website name. Provide a summary comparing the different perspectives.

details.url: <URL from search result #2 (different domain from result #1)>
details.intent: Open a second recent news article from a different publisher (different domain than Article A) and extract headline, publication date, main topic/key point, and source name.

target_facts.headline: string (exact headline text)
target_facts.publication_date: ISO date string or article-provided date
target_facts.main_topic: 1-2 sentence summary (policy angle, e.g., regulatory proposal, industry reaction, enforcement focus)

details.extraction_plan: From the second article page extract the same fields as for Article A. Also capture any quoted perspectives (government, industry, civil society) that reveal the article's stance or emphasis.
verification_steps: Confirm this article's domain is different from Article A to meet the 'at least 2 different news websites' requirement.
Confirm the publication date is within the past 12 months; if not, fallback to the next search result.
Cross-check any policy names or facts with Article A to prepare for a comparative summary.
details.rationale: Visiting a second publisher ensures we capture different editorial perspectives and reduces bias from a single source. Extracting quoted stakeholders and the article emphasis supports a robust comparison.
